name: Western Sahara (Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic / الجمهورية العربية الصحراوية الديمقراطية / SADR)
summary: Western Sahara is a North African mixed-legal-system disputed territory — structurally distinctive globally as the only remaining UN-listed non-self-governing territory pending decolonisation in Africa. Approximately 80% of the territory is administered by Morocco (which considers it the Southern Provinces under Moroccan Family Code 2004 Moudawana framework); the remaining ~20% (Free Zone) is controlled by the Polisario Front (Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic / SADR, recognised by 47 UN member states and African Union). Family-law framework in Moroccan-administered area operates under the Moroccan Moudawana 2004 (Maliki-Islamic-law); SADR-administered area operates under SADR Personal Status Code drawing on Maliki-Islamic-law and Hassaniya customary-law (Hassaniya being the Sahrawi dialect of Arabic). Parental authority and child custody are governed by Moudawana arts. 163-186 (Moroccan-administered) or SADR Personal Status Code (SADR-administered) and customary-law for matters not addressed. The Sahrawi judicial framework operates within SADR (apex Supreme Court in Tindouf-area refugee camps) and Moroccan judicial framework operates in Moroccan-administered area. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Moroccan-administered) and Sahrawi District Courts (SADR-administered). Western Sahara is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Western Sahara is not a Hague Convention 1980 party; Morocco's Hague accession (effective 2010) covers Moroccan-administered area but SADR Free Zone is non-Hague.

statutory_framework.title: Moroccan Moudawana — Custody and guardianship
statutory_framework.relevance: Moroccan Family Code applied in Moroccan-administered area of Western Sahara — Maliki Islamic-law framework with substantial 2004 modernisation.

statutory_framework.title: SADR Personal Status Code
statutory_framework.relevance: SADR Personal Status Code applied in SADR-administered Free Zone — Maliki Islamic-law + Hassaniya customary-law framework.

apex_courts.name: Moroccan Court of Cassation (Moroccan-administered area)
apex_courts.role: Apex Moroccan court for civil and criminal matters covering Moroccan-administered Western Sahara.

apex_courts.name: Sahrawi Supreme Court (SADR-administered area)
apex_courts.role: Apex SADR court for SADR-administered Free Zone.

professional_regulators.name: Various — Moroccan Ministry of Health (Moroccan-administered) / SADR Ministry of Health (SADR-administered)
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulators of health professionals operating in respective administered areas.

anonymisation_convention: Western Sahara family-court decisions are anonymised per relevant court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Spanish-protectorate-establishment + Rio-de-Oro framework
key_developments.description: Spanish protectorate over Rio de Oro established 26 December 1884 at Berlin Conference — substantively distinctive North African Spanish-Sahara colonial framework. Foundational pre-1975-Madrid-Accords framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Polisario Front founded + Sahrawi-self-determination framework
key_developments.description: Frente POLISARIO founded 10 May 1973 by Sahrawi students at Zouirate — substantively distinctive North African Sahrawi-self-determination-movement framework. Foundational pre-1975-Spanish-withdrawal framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Spanish withdrawal + Madrid Accords + Green-March framework
key_developments.description: ICJ Advisory Opinion 16 October 1975 + Green March 6 November 1975 (350,000 Moroccans marching into Spanish Sahara) + Madrid Accords 14 November 1975 — substantively distinctive globally only-mass-civilian-territorial-march-followed-by-multi-state-administration-transfer framework. Spanish colonial withdrawal; Madrid Accords transferred administration to Morocco and Mauritania (Mauritania subsequently withdrew 1979). Foundational substantive North African Western-Sahara-dispute framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: SADR declaration + Polisario-Sahrawi-Arab-Democratic-Republic framework
key_developments.description: Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic (SADR) declared by Polisario Front 27 February 1976 — substantively distinctive globally partial-recognition-by-47-UN-member-states-+-African-Union framework. Foundational substantive 49+ year SADR de-facto-state framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Ceasefire + MINURSO + UN-supervised-referendum-pending framework
key_developments.description: Ceasefire between Morocco and Polisario 6 September 1991 — substantively distinctive globally UN-Mission-for-Referendum-in-Western-Sahara-MINURSO-self-determination-referendum-pending-34-years framework. Substantive UN-supervised-referendum-pending framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Morocco ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Western-Sahara-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Morocco ratified UNCRC 21 June 1993 with applicability to Moroccan-administered Western Sahara — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Maliki-Islamic-derivative framework applicable to Moroccan-administered Western Sahara.

key_developments.title: Moroccan Moudawana 2004 + family-law-modernisation framework
key_developments.description: Moroccan Family Code (Moudawana) 2004 substantially modernising Maliki-Islamic-law framework applicable in Moroccan-administered Western Sahara — substantively distinctive North African Moudawana-modernisation framework. Substantive family-law-implementation framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 Morocco accession + split-Hague-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Morocco acceded to Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 June 2010 covering Moroccan-administered Western Sahara per Morocco's territorial claim. Substantively distinctive globally split-Hague-applicability-within-single-disputed-territory framework with SADR Free Zone non-Hague. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Resumption of armed conflict + Trump-Morocco-recognition-+-Abraham-Accords framework
key_developments.description: Polisario-Morocco ceasefire broke down 13 November 2020 — substantively distinctive North African armed-conflict-resumption framework. US President Trump recognition of Moroccan sovereignty over Western Sahara 10 December 2020 in exchange for Morocco-Israel normalisation (Abraham Accords) — substantively distinctive globally US-recognition-as-quid-pro-quo-for-Israel-normalisation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: Continuing armed-conflict framework + UN MINURSO continuing-mission + Sahrawi refugee-camps framework
key_developments.description: Continuing Polisario-Morocco armed-conflict framework 2020-2023 substantively significant institutional context affecting demographic and family-law adjudication. UN MINURSO continuing-mission substantively significant institutional context. Tindouf-area Sahrawi refugee-camps framework substantively significant for SADR family-law adjudication. Algeria-Morocco diplomatic-rupture (August 2021) framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Maghreb disputed-territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Moroccan Court of Cassation + Sahrawi Supreme Court — split-jurisdiction substantive register + continuing-UN-non-self-governing-territory framework
key_developments.description: Moroccan Court of Cassation (Moroccan-administered) and Sahrawi Supreme Court (SADR-administered, Tindouf-area refugee camps) continue to develop family-law jurisprudence under Moudawana 2004 (Moroccan-administered) and SADR Personal Status Code (SADR-administered) within continuing-UN-non-self-governing-territory framework. Subsequent withdrawals/reaffirmations of recognition by various states. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Western Sahara is structurally distinctive globally within the corpus as the only remaining UN-listed non-self-governing territory pending decolonisation in Africa.

Dual legal-system administration (Moroccan + SADR) within a single UN-recognised territory is structurally distinctive globally — only state in the corpus with concurrent dual-administration legal frameworks.

Hague Convention 1980 applicability is split — Morocco's accession covers Moroccan-administered area; SADR Free Zone is non-Hague. Structurally distinctive split-Hague-applicability status within a single territory.
SADR recognition by 47 UN member states + African Union but not UN seat itself is structurally distinctive partial-recognition status.
editorial_notes: Western Sahara jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Spanish-protectorate-1884-to-Trump-Morocco-recognition trajectory: 1884-Spanish-protectorate-establishment-+-Rio-de-Oro + 1973-Polisario-Front-founded + 1975-Spanish-withdrawal-+-Madrid-Accords-+-Green-March + 1976-SADR-declaration + 1991-ceasefire-+-MINURSO + 1993-Morocco-UNCRC-ratification + 2004-Moroccan-Moudawana + 2010-Hague-Convention-Morocco-accession + 2020-resumption-of-armed-conflict-+-Trump-Morocco-recognition-+-Abraham-Accords + 2024-Moroccan-Court-of-Cassation-+-Sahrawi-Supreme-Court-split-jurisdiction.

Mixed-legal-system North African UN non-self-governing territory (Moroccan Moudawana 2004 + SADR Personal Status Code 1991 + Hassaniya customary + dual-administration framework + split-Hague-applicability + partial-recognition SADR). Only remaining UN-listed African non-self-governing territory pending decolonisation + UN-supervised-referendum-pending-34-years.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive family-law analysis under Moroccan Moudawana 2004 (Moroccan-administered) or SADR Personal Status Code 1991 (SADR-administered) and customary-law without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-African + mixed-legal-system + UN-non-self-governing-territory-pending-decolonisation-only-remaining-in-Africa-globally-distinctive + dual-administration-Moroccan-+-SADR-globally-distinctive + partial-recognition-SADR-47-UN-member-states-+-African-Union + split-Hague-applicability-globally-distinctive + Spanish-protectorate-1884 + Polisario-Front-founded-1973 + Green-March-1975-only-mass-civilian-territorial-march-globally-distinctive + Madrid-Accords-1975 + SADR-declaration-1976 + ceasefire-1991-+-MINURSO + Moroccan-Moudawana-2004 + Hague-Convention-Morocco-accession-2010 + 2020-armed-conflict-resumption + Trump-Morocco-recognition-+-Abraham-Accords-2020-globally-distinctive clusters within the corpus.
